snp3.9:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying at Icchānaṅgala in the Icchānaṅgala forest grove.
snp3.9:1.3: And on that occasion many well-known wealthy brahmans were dwelling in Icchānaṅgala,
snp3.9:1.4:
i.e., Caṅkī the brahman, Tārukkha the brahman, Pokkharasāti the brahman,
Jānusoṇin the brahman, Todeyya the brahman, and many other well-known wealthy brahmans.
snp3.9:1.5:
Then, while the young brahmans Vāseṭṭha and Bhāradvāja were walking and wandering about to exercise their legs,
this conversation arose between them:
snp3.9:2.2:
“When one is well-born on both sides, the mother’s & the father’s,
is of pure descent for seven generations of fathers—uncriticized & irreproachable in the telling of one’s birth:
snp3.9:2.3: It’s to that extent that one is a brahman.”
snp3.9:3.3: “When one is virtuous & consummate in one’s practices, it’s to that extent that one is a brahman.”
snp3.9:3.4:
But neither was the young brahman Bhāradvāja able to win over the young brahman Vāseṭṭha,
nor was the young brahman Vāseṭṭha able to win over the young brahman Bhāradvāja.
snp3.9:4.1: Then the young brahman Vāseṭṭha said to the young brahman Bhāradvāja,
snp3.9:4.2:
“Bhāradvāja, this Gotama the contemplative—a son of the Sakyans,
gone forth from the Sakyan clan—is staying at Icchānaṅgala in the Icchānaṅgala forest grove.
snp3.9:4.3: And of that Master Gotama this fine reputation has spread:
snp3.9:4.4:
‘He is indeed a Blessed One, worthy & rightly self-awakened, consummate in clear-knowing & conduct, well-gone,
an expert with regard to the cosmos, unexcelled trainer of people fit to be tamed,
teacher of devas & human beings, awakened, blessed.’
snp3.9:4.5: Come, let’s go to Gotama the contemplative and, on arrival, ask him about this matter.
snp3.9:4.6: However he answers, that’s how we’ll hold it.”
snp3.9:4.7: “As you say, master,” the young brahman Bhāradvāja responded to the young brahman Vāseṭṭha.
snp3.9:5.1:
So the young brahmans Vāseṭṭha and Bhāradvāja went to the Blessed One and,
on arrival, exchanged courteous greetings with him.
snp3.9:5.2: After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, they sat to one side.
snp3.9:5.3: As they were sitting there, the young brahman Vāseṭṭha addressed the Blessed One in verses:
snp3.9:6.2: as masters of the three knowledges:
snp3.9:7.1: Whatever is taught by masters of the three knowledges,
snp3.9:7.4: on a par with our teachers in recitation.
snp3.9:8.3: Bhāradvāja says that one is a brahman
snp3.9:9.1: Neither of us can win over the other.
snp3.9:10.2: with hands palm-to-palm over the heart
snp3.9:10.3: pay homage, venerating the moon when it’s just past new,
snp3.9:10.4: in the same way in the world, Gotama,
snp3.9:11.1: we ask Gotama, the Eye arisen in the world:
snp3.9:11.3: Is one a brahman through birth
snp3.9:11.6:
how we might know a brahman.”
The Buddha:
snp3.9:12.5: for their species differ, one from another.
snp3.9:13.2: even though they don’t proclaim themselves:
snp3.9:13.3: Their distinguishing markings are made by nature,
snp3.9:13.4: for their species differ, one from another.
snp3.9:14.1: Then beetles & moths, down to white ants:
snp3.9:14.3: Their distinguishing markings are made by nature,
snp3.9:14.4: for their species differ, one from another.
snp3.9:15.3: Their distinguishing markings are made by nature,
snp3.9:15.4: for their species differ, one from another.
snp3.9:16.3: Their distinguishing markings are made by nature,
snp3.9:16.4: for their species differ, one from another.
snp3.9:17.1: Then you know fish in the water, with water their range:
snp3.9:17.3: Their distinguishing markings are made by nature,
snp3.9:17.4: for their species differ, one from another.
snp3.9:18.1: Then you know birds, with wings as their vehicles, coursing through the sky:
snp3.9:18.3: Their distinguishing markings are made by nature,
snp3.9:18.4: for their species differ, one from another.
snp3.9:19.2: have many distinguishing marks made by nature,
snp3.9:19.4: have many distinguishing marks made by nature:
snp3.9:22.5: Their distinguishing mark is not made by nature
snp3.9:22.6: as it is for other species.
snp3.9:23.1: In human beings that’s not found
snp3.9:24.2: makes a living by guarding cows,
snp3.9:24.4: as a farmer, not as a brahman.
snp3.9:25.2: makes a living through various crafts,
snp3.9:25.4: as a craftsman, not as a brahman.
snp3.9:26.4: as a merchant, not as a brahman.
snp3.9:27.2: makes a living by serving others,
snp3.9:27.4: as a servant, not as a brahman.
snp3.9:28.4: This is a thief, not a brahman.
snp3.9:29.2: makes a living through arrow & sword,
snp3.9:29.4: as a soldier, not as a brahman.
snp3.9:30.4: as a sacrificer, not as a brahman.
snp3.9:31.2: makes a living partaking of city & state,
snp3.9:31.4: as a king, not as a brahman.
snp3.9:32.1: I don’t call one a brahman
snp3.9:32.2: for being born of a mother or sprung from a womb.
snp3.9:32.4: if he has anything at all.
snp3.9:32.5: But someone with nothing, who clings to no thing:
snp3.9:32.6: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:33.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:34.3: having thrown off the bar, awakened:
snp3.9:34.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:35.3:
His army is strength;
his strength, forbearance:
snp3.9:35.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:36.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:37.1: Like water on a lotus leaf,
snp3.9:37.2: a mustard seed on the tip of an awl,
snp3.9:37.3: he doesn’t adhere to sensual pleasures:
snp3.9:37.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:38.1: He discerns right here, for himself, on his own,
snp3.9:38.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:39.2: as to what is the path & what’s not;
snp3.9:39.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:40.3: living with no home, with next to no wants:
snp3.9:40.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:41.3: he neither kills nor gets others to kill:
snp3.9:41.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:42.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:43.3: like a mustard seed from the tip of an awl:
snp3.9:43.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:44.1: He would say what’s non-grating, instructive,
snp3.9:44.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:45.3: Here in the world he takes nothing not-given —long, short, large, small, attractive, not:
snp3.9:45.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:46.1: His longing for this & for the next world
snp3.9:46.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:47.1: His attachments, his homes, can’t be found.
snp3.9:47.3: has reached a footing in the deathless:
snp3.9:47.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:48.1: He has gone beyond attachment here
snp3.9:48.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:49.3: his delights, his becomings, totally gone:
snp3.9:49.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:50.1: He has made his way past this hard-going path:
snp3.9:50.3: He’s crossed over, has gone beyond,
snp3.9:50.4: is free from want, from perplexity,
snp3.9:50.5: absorbed in jhāna, through no-clinging unbound:
snp3.9:50.6: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:51.3: his sensual passions, becomings, totally gone:
snp3.9:51.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:52.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:53.1: Having left behind the human bond,
snp3.9:53.2: having made his way past the divine,
snp3.9:53.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:54.3: a hero who has conquered all the world, every world:
snp3.9:54.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:55.2: beings’ passing away, and their re- arising;
snp3.9:55.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:56.1: He whose course they don’t know
snp3.9:56.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:57.3: the one with nothing who clings to no thing:
snp3.9:57.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:58.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:59.2: He sees heavens & states of woe,
snp3.9:59.3: has attained the ending of birth:
snp3.9:59.4: He’s what I call a brahman.
snp3.9:60.1: For this is a convention in the world:
snp3.9:60.3: come into being from common consent,
snp3.9:61.4: say that one is a brahman by birth.
snp3.9:62.1: Not by birth is one a brahman,
snp3.9:62.3: By action is one a brahman.
snp3.9:62.4: By action one is a non-brahman.
snp3.9:63.1: By action is one a farmer.
snp3.9:63.2: By action one is a craftsman.
snp3.9:63.3: By action is one a merchant.
snp3.9:63.4: By action one is a servant.
snp3.9:64.1: By action is one a thief.
snp3.9:64.2: By action one is a soldier.
snp3.9:64.3: By action is one a sacrificer.
snp3.9:64.4: By action one is a king.
snp3.9:65.1: The wise see action in this way
snp3.9:65.2: as it has come to be,
snp3.9:66.1: Through action the world rolls on.
snp3.9:66.3: In action are beings held bound together,
snp3.9:66.4: as in a linchpin, a chariot traveling along.
snp3.9:67.3: That’s how one is a brahman.
snp3.9:68.4: That’s Brahmā, that’s Sakka, for those who directly know.”
snp3.9:69.1: When this was said, the young brahmans Vāseṭṭha & Bhāradvāja said to the Blessed One:
snp3.9:69.2:
“Magnificent, Master Gotama!
Magnificent!
Just as if he were to place upright what was overturned, to reveal what was hidden, to show the way to one who was lost,
or to carry a lamp into the dark so that those with eyes could see forms,
in the same way has Master Gotama—through many lines of reasoning—made the Dhamma clear.
We go to Master Gotama for refuge, to the Dhamma, & to the Saṅgha of monks.
snp3.9:69.3: May Master Gotama remember us as lay followers who have gone for refuge from this day forward, for life.”
